meta.note: This dataset defines the structure every layer/player record uses. Numeric financial fields are intentionally left as 'pending live refresh' rather than populated with placeholder numbers, to avoid presenting any unverified figure as fact. Once deployed and connected to Finnhub, SEC EDGAR, World Bank, IMF, Eurostat, Stanford HAI, and WEF per the architecture, the Netlify Functions in /netlify/functions populate these fields live.

layers.name: Semiconductors & AI Chip Design
hoverDescription: Companies that design the processors (GPUs, AI accelerators, custom silicon) that power AI training and inference.
Distinct from manufacturing — these firms design chips but typically don't fabricate them.
layers.overview: Designs the compute primitives — GPUs, TPUs, NPUs, and custom AI accelerators — that every other layer of the stack ultimately runs on.

subComponents.name: GPU & AI Accelerators
subComponents.description: General-purpose and AI-specific parallel processors used for training and inference.

subComponents.name: Custom Silicon (ASIC/NPU)
subComponents.description: Purpose-built chips designed for a specific AI workload or company.

subComponents.name: Chip-to-Chip Interconnect
subComponents.description: High-speed links (e.g. NVLink-class fabrics) that let many chips act as one cluster.

players.name: AMD
players.keyProducts: Instinct AI accelerators, EPYC server CPUs, Ryzen CPUs

players.name: Intel
players.keyProducts: Xeon server CPUs, Gaudi AI accelerators, Core CPUs

players.name: Qualcomm
players.keyProducts: Snapdragon mobile/AI chips, Cloud AI accelerators

players.name: Broadcom
players.keyProducts: Custom AI ASICs (XPUs), networking silicon, VMware software

layers.name: Chip Fabrication & Manufacturing Equipment
hoverDescription: Foundries that physically manufacture chips, plus makers of the equipment used to fabricate them.
A separate market from chip design, with different economics, players, and capital cycles.
layers.overview: Turns chip designs into physical silicon — the foundries and the lithography/equipment makers that supply them.

subComponents.name: Advanced-Node Foundries
subComponents.description: Contract manufacturers that fabricate chips designed by other companies.

subComponents.name: Lithography & Fab Equipment
subComponents.description: The machinery foundries depend on to print circuits at advanced nodes.

subComponents.name: Advanced Packaging
subComponents.description: Techniques (e.g. chiplet packaging) that combine multiple dies into one package.

players.name: TSMC
players.keyProducts: N2/N3/N5 process nodes, CoWoS advanced packaging, TSMC Foundry Services

layers.name: Cloud & AI Infrastructure
hoverDescription: Hyperscale compute providers and data-center operators that host AI training/inference workloads.
Includes traditional hyperscalers and newer AI-specific compute providers.
layers.overview: Provides the data-center scale compute, storage, and networking that AI workloads actually run on, rented by the hour or committed contract.

subComponents.name: Hyperscale Cloud Platforms
subComponents.description: Full-service cloud providers offering AI compute alongside their broader cloud catalog.

subComponents.name: Specialist GPU Cloud
subComponents.description: Providers focused specifically on renting AI accelerator capacity.

subComponents.name: Data Center Operations & Power
subComponents.description: Physical facility build-out, cooling, and energy supply for AI compute clusters.

players.name: Microsoft (Azure)
players.keyProducts: Azure cloud platform, Azure AI services

players.name: Google Cloud
players.keyProducts: Google Cloud Platform, Vertex AI, TPU silicon

players.name: CoreWeave
players.keyProducts: GPU cloud infrastructure, AI Object Storage

layers.name: Data Infrastructure & Management
hoverDescription: Platforms for storing, processing, and retrieving the data that trains and feeds AI systems, including vector databases.
Underpins both model training and real-time AI application performance.
layers.overview: Stores, organizes, and serves the data — structured and unstructured — that AI models are trained and grounded on.

subComponents.name: Data Warehousing & Lakes
subComponents.description: Large-scale storage and querying of structured and semi-structured data.

subComponents.name: Vector & Embedding Databases
subComponents.description: Specialized storage for the numerical representations AI models use for retrieval.

subComponents.name: Data Labeling & Curation
subComponents.description: Preparing and annotating data for model training.

players.name: Snowflake
players.keyProducts: Cloud data warehousing, Snowflake Cortex AI

layers.name: Foundation Models / Frontier AI Labs
hoverDescription: Organizations that build and release large-scale AI models (language, multimodal, etc.).
The “intelligence layer” — the core models other layers build on top of.
layers.overview: Trains and releases the large-scale models — language, vision, multimodal — that act as the reasoning core for the rest of the stack.

subComponents.name: Large Language Models
subComponents.description: General-purpose text and reasoning models.

subComponents.name: Multimodal Models
subComponents.description: Models spanning text, image, audio, and video.

subComponents.name: Open-Weight Models
subComponents.description: Models released with publicly downloadable weights.

players.name: Google DeepMind
players.keyProducts: Gemini AI models, AlphaFold, Veo video generation, AI research

players.name: Meta AI
players.keyProducts: Llama open-weight models, Meta AI assistant, PyTorch framework

layers.name: MLOps & Model Development Tooling
hoverDescription: Platforms for training, fine-tuning, evaluating, and monitoring AI models through their lifecycle.
Bridges raw models and production-ready deployment.
layers.overview: Provides the tooling teams use to fine-tune, evaluate, version, and monitor models from experiment to production.

subComponents.name: Model Hubs & Registries
subComponents.description: Repositories for sharing and versioning pretrained and fine-tuned models.

subComponents.name: Experiment Tracking
subComponents.description: Tools for logging and comparing training runs.

subComponents.name: Evaluation & Monitoring
subComponents.description: Tools for testing model quality and tracking production performance.

layers.name: AI Agent & Orchestration Layer
hoverDescription: Frameworks that connect models to external tools, data, and multi-step workflows (agents, RAG pipelines).
A distinct, fast-growing segment separate from the underlying foundation models themselves.
layers.overview: Connects raw models to tools, memory, and external data so they can carry out multi-step tasks rather than answer single prompts.

subComponents.name: Agent Frameworks
subComponents.description: Libraries for building multi-step, tool-using AI agents.

subComponents.name: RAG Pipelines
subComponents.description: Systems that retrieve external knowledge to ground model responses.

subComponents.name: Orchestration Middleware
subComponents.description: Infrastructure for chaining prompts, tools, and models into workflows.

layers.name: Application Layer
hoverDescription: Enterprise and consumer-facing products that embed AI to deliver a specific end-user function.
Where AI capability is ultimately monetized as a finished product or service.
layers.overview: The finished products — enterprise and consumer — where AI capability is packaged into something a person actually uses.

subComponents.name: Enterprise AI Applications
subComponents.description: AI embedded into business software (CRM, ITSM, productivity suites).

subComponents.name: Consumer AI Applications
subComponents.description: AI-first products aimed directly at individual end users.

subComponents.name: Vertical AI (industry-specific)
subComponents.description: AI applications built for a specific industry, e.g. legal, healthcare, finance.

players.name: ServiceNow
players.keyProducts: Now Platform, AI Agents for ITSM/HR/CSM

layers.name: AI Governance, Safety & Compliance Tooling
hoverDescription: Vendors building model evaluation, red-teaming, observability, and regulatory-compliance software for AI systems.
A market segment in its own right, distinct from policy/regulatory bodies themselves.
layers.overview: Builds the software companies use to test, monitor, and document AI systems for safety and regulatory compliance — a vendor market, not the policymakers themselves.

subComponents.name: Model Evaluation & Red-Teaming
subComponents.description: Tools for systematically testing model behavior and vulnerabilities.

subComponents.name: AI Observability
subComponents.description: Monitoring AI systems in production for drift, errors, and misuse.

subComponents.name: Regulatory Compliance Software
subComponents.description: Tools for documenting and demonstrating compliance with AI regulation.
